entries.title: Indoor preliminary alarm C5
entries.meaning: Preliminary diagnostic guidance for a Gree indoor unit C5 alarm. Verify the exact meaning against the service manual for the installed indoor unit family before drawing a final conclusion.
likelyCauses: Indoor controller, communication, addressing, or configuration condition reported by the unit.
Loose communication terminal, incorrect addressing, or controller configuration mismatch.
Model-specific interpretation that requires exact manual verification.

diagnosticSteps.title: Confirm indoor unit identity
diagnosticSteps.instruction: Record indoor unit model, controller type, displayed C5 code, connected outdoor unit, and recent commissioning changes.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Indoor unit identity and communication context are documented before diagnosis continues.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Stop classification and obtain exact indoor model and controller information.

diagnosticSteps.title: Inspect communication and addressing
diagnosticSteps.instruction: A qualified technician should inspect communication terminals, addressing, controller configuration, and wiring continuity according to the service manual.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Communication wiring and addressing match the installed system topology.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Correct communication or configuration issues using supported commissioning procedures.

requiredMeasurements.name: Communication bus condition
requiredMeasurements.description: Communication voltage or controller diagnostic value required by the exact service procedure.

requiredMeasurements.name: Address configuration
requiredMeasurements.description: Indoor controller and unit address settings recorded from commissioning information.

safetyNotes: Electrical and controller checks must be performed by a qualified technician.
Keep manufacturer safeguards, safety inputs, and controller protections active during diagnosis.
Verify the exact service manual before drawing a final diagnosis.

source.notes: Seeded deterministic diagnostic guidance. Not manually page-verified.
limitations: Use as preliminary diagnostic guidance only.
Verify against the exact service manual for the installed equipment family before final conclusion.
Indoor, duct, and split-family code meanings can vary by installed controller and model.

entries.title: Indoor preliminary alarm E1
entries.meaning: Preliminary diagnostic guidance for a Gree indoor unit E1 alarm. Use only as seed guidance until exact manual evidence is added for the installed indoor model.
likelyCauses: Indoor control, communication, or protection condition requiring model-specific confirmation.
Power supply, terminal, sensor, or controller input issue requiring qualified diagnosis.
Code meaning may differ between indoor, duct, and split equipment families.

diagnosticSteps.title: Record displayed code and context
diagnosticSteps.instruction: Record indoor unit model, controller, displayed E1 code, operating mode, and connected system information.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Equipment identity and displayed code are confirmed before classification.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Stop classification and obtain exact model/controller information.

diagnosticSteps.title: Check supply and controller condition
diagnosticSteps.instruction: A qualified technician should verify supply condition, controller wiring, and relevant sensor/controller readings according to the exact service manual.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Supply and controller values are plausible for the active operating state.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Follow the manufacturer diagnostic path for wiring, sensor, or controller issues.

requiredMeasurements.name: Indoor unit supply voltage
requiredMeasurements.description: Measured supply voltage at the indoor unit service point by qualified personnel.

requiredMeasurements.name: Controller diagnostic value
requiredMeasurements.description: Relevant controller value for the suspected indoor E1 condition.

safetyNotes: Electrical and controller checks must be performed by a qualified technician.
Keep manufacturer safeguards, safety inputs, and controller protections active during diagnosis.
Verify the exact service manual before drawing a final diagnosis.

source.notes: Seeded deterministic diagnostic guidance. Not manually page-verified.
limitations: Use as preliminary diagnostic guidance only.
Verify against the exact service manual for the installed equipment family before final conclusion.
Indoor, duct, and split-family code meanings can vary by installed controller and model.

entries.title: Indoor preliminary alarm F0
entries.meaning: Preliminary diagnostic guidance for a Gree indoor unit F0 alarm. Confirm exact code meaning and sensor association from the installed model service manual.
likelyCauses: Indoor temperature sensor, coil sensor, or controller input condition reported by the unit.
Loose sensor terminal, unstable sensor reading, or airflow/heat-exchange condition.
Model-specific sensor assignment that must be verified against the exact manual.

diagnosticSteps.title: Confirm sensor context
diagnosticSteps.instruction: Record indoor model, displayed F0 code, return air condition, operating mode, and controller-reported sensor values.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Sensor context and operating condition are documented before classification.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Collect exact model and controller diagnostic data before continuing.

diagnosticSteps.title: Compare indoor readings
diagnosticSteps.instruction: A qualified technician should compare controller temperature readings with measured indoor conditions using the exact service procedure.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Sensor readings are plausible and stable for the observed condition.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Follow the manufacturer diagnostic path for sensor, wiring, or airflow issues.

requiredMeasurements.name: Controller temperature reading
requiredMeasurements.description: Relevant controller-reported indoor temperature or coil value.

requiredMeasurements.name: Measured indoor reference temperature
requiredMeasurements.description: Reference temperature measured near the relevant indoor location by qualified personnel.

safetyNotes: Electrical and controller checks must be performed by a qualified technician.
Keep manufacturer safeguards, safety inputs, and controller protections active during diagnosis.
Verify the exact service manual before drawing a final diagnosis.

source.notes: Seeded deterministic diagnostic guidance. Not manually page-verified.
limitations: Use as preliminary diagnostic guidance only.
Verify against the exact service manual for the installed equipment family before final conclusion.
Indoor, duct, and split-family code meanings can vary by installed controller and model.

entries.title: Indoor preliminary alarm H6
entries.meaning: Preliminary diagnostic guidance for a Gree indoor unit H6 alarm. Verify exact meaning against the service manual for the installed indoor, duct, or split-family model before final diagnosis.
likelyCauses: Indoor fan, airflow, motor feedback, or controller input condition requiring model-specific confirmation.
Restricted indoor airflow, fan wiring issue, or controller input issue.
Model-specific fan or airflow protection interpretation that requires qualified diagnosis.

diagnosticSteps.title: Confirm fan and airflow context
diagnosticSteps.instruction: Record indoor model, displayed H6 code, fan command state, filter condition, and observed airflow condition.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Fan and airflow context are documented before diagnosis continues.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Collect exact model and controller data before component-level diagnosis.

diagnosticSteps.title: Inspect indoor airflow path
diagnosticSteps.instruction: A qualified technician should inspect filter condition, coil cleanliness, fan operation, wiring, and controller indications according to the service manual.
diagnosticSteps.expectedResult: Indoor airflow path and fan-related wiring are suitable for the operating state.
diagnosticSteps.ifFailedAction: Correct airflow, fan, wiring, or controller issues using the approved service process.

requiredMeasurements.name: Fan command state
requiredMeasurements.description: Controller command or operating state for the indoor fan.

requiredMeasurements.name: Airflow path condition
requiredMeasurements.description: Filter, coil, and airflow path condition observed by qualified personnel.

safetyNotes: Electrical and controller checks must be performed by a qualified technician.
Keep manufacturer safeguards, safety inputs, and controller protections active during diagnosis.
Verify the exact service manual before drawing a final diagnosis.
